title: Innodata Stock Jumps on AI Revenue Beat: The $51 Million Big Tech Deal Driving INOD Higher
text: Innodata Inc. experienced a dramatic stock surge of approximately 89% following strong first-quarter results and raised 2026 guidance. The AI data-engineering company reported Q1 revenue of $90.1 million, representing 54% year-over-year growth. The standout catalyst was a major Big Tech customer that generated no revenue in the prior year but is projected to become the company's second-largest account by 2026, potentially contributing around $51 million annually. Revenue from the broader Big Tech customer base jumped 453% during the quarter. Full-year 2026 revenue growth guidance increased to at least 40% (up from 35%). Adjusted EPS of 42 cents exceeded consensus estimates of 17 cents. Adjusted EBITDA reached $25.0 million (28% of revenue), doubling year-over-year. Adjusted gross margin expanded to 47%. The company is diversifying its AI offerings beyond traditional data services, including new agent observability platform initiatives and federal contracts through partnerships with Palantir and involvement in the Missile Defense Agency's SHIELD program. Risk Factor: One client represented 56% of Q1 revenue, highlighting customer concentration vulnerability.
